title: podman cannot be run in amd64 container running under OSX arm processor with rosetta
body:
### Issue Description

I've created a UBI9 based container to use in certain contexts, with podman installed (dnf install -y podman).  I am on an Arm mac as everyone will be someday, running containers using Docker Desktop.  I'm building x86-64 containers since that's the target platform, so I run them under Rosetta on osx.

The apparent way that (Docker Desktop) integration happens is somehow using rosetta to run every command.  Apparently `podman` is looking at some absolute count to get the command argument and is failing.  The scenario below shows this

    $ uname -a
    Darwin marvins-laptop.lan 23.3.0 Darwin Kernel Version 23.3.0: Wed Dec 20 21:30:44 PST 2023; root:xnu-10002.81.5~7/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 arm64

    $ docker run -u root -e --platform  linux/amd64 -it builder-jenkins:ubi9.2.v3

    builder_user ~ $ rpm -q podman
    podman-4.6.1-7.el9_3.x86_64
    builder_user ~ $ pwd
    /home/builder_user
    builder_user ~ $ podman info
    Error: unrecognized command `podman podman`
    Try 'podman --help' for more information
    builder_user ~ $ podman --version
    podman version 4.6.1
    builder_user ~ $ podman info& podman info& ps -ef& podman info&
    UID        PID  PPID  C STIME TTY          TIME CMD
    root         1     0  0 11:20 pts/0    00:00:00 /rosetta/rosetta /usr/bin/su su builder_user -- -c bash
    builder+    64     1  0 11:20 pts/0    00:00:00 /rosetta/rosetta /usr/bin/bash bash
    builder+   110     1  0 11:20 ?        00:00:00 /rosetta/rosetta /usr/libexec/podman/catatonit catatonit -P
    builder+   153    64  0 11:21 pts/0    00:00:00 /rosetta/rosetta /usr/bin/podman podman info
    builder+   154    64  0 11:21 pts/0    00:00:00 /rosetta/rosetta /usr/bin/podman podman info
    builder+   155    64  0 11:21 pts/0    00:00:00 /usr/bin/ps ps -ef


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Build a UBI9 container target, for target arch linux/amd64, with podman installed
2. On an ARM OSX system with rosetta installed and integrated into docker desktop, run the container
3. run `podman info`

A simple dockerfile is below.

    FROM --platform=linux/amd64 registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi:9.2
    RUN dnf update -y && dnf install -y podman procps-ng
    RUN useradd -U builder_user --shell /bin/bash
    USER builder_user
    WORKDIR /home/builder_user/
    CMD [ "bash","-l" ]


### Describe the results you received

$ podman info
    Error: unrecognized command `podman podman`

### Describe the results you expected

I expect it to run.  I understand that podman in this environment may not work properly - for example starting `podman machine` may fail, other things may not function.  Interestingly this behavior is NOT observed for `podman machine info`, etc.   For example, `podman machine init` correctly parses the command line, and reports that it cannot start a VM.


### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.

UBI9 container, linux amd64 arch 0n OSX Arm64
```


### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Looks like your setup is broken, `podman podman` is not a valid command so we error correctly.
Your ps output even shows `/usr/bin/su su ...` and `/usr/bin/bash bash` so this is not related to podman at all, it is just a coincidence that bash and su works as they just execute themselves again.
